blocks.content: Shirone was too out of breath to respond.
He had been observing the situation from afar but rushed over at full speed when things became critical.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can you just kill someone without warning? I don't know what's going on, but can't you resolve this through dialogue?

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: What? Kill someone? How dare you reduce the great cause of revolution to mere murder!
Aria recognized Shirone's face.
He was the boy she had felt a liking for during their encounter in the carriage a few days ago.
But now was no time for such thoughts.
Tears and snot streaming down her face, Aria pleaded with Shirone, her only hope.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: Please save me! Please, I beg you!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shut up! The revolution has already begun!
As a scythe was placed against Aria's neck, Shirone acted quickly and resourcefully.
Clutching a metal box to his side, he raised his hand and shouted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait! In that case, take me as your hostage instead!
The scythe stopped just before it could pierce Aria's throat.
The revolutionary had trained for three years to complete their grand cause, but they had never encountered such a situation before.
Who would willingly risk their life for a stranger?

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: You want us to take you as a hostage?
Shirone pulled out his Magic Association ID from his inner pocket, covering Plu's picture with his thumb, and held it out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As you can see, I'm an employee of the Magic Association. Taking me hostage would be much more effective than a civilian.
The revolutionary finally caught on to Shirone's ploy.
It was a stalling tactic.
They had never seen a Magic Association ID before, and verifying it here was impossible.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Hmph! You're a Magic Association employee? You, a kid? Do you really think I'd believe that?
At that moment, a terrorist standing by the window, who had been observing Shirone carefully, spoke.

blocks.speaker: Terrorist
blocks.content: Bring that kid over.
All the revolutionaries in the Gold Tower turned their attention to him.
Though his face was hidden beneath a hood, the atmosphere made it clear that they were puzzled.

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: Captain, do you really believe him?

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: It's not about believing him. He is a Magic Association employee. I confirmed it at Akim Trading Company. If we capture a Magic Association employee as a hostage, our demands will be much easier to meet.
One of the revolutionaries relayed the captain's message through the window.
Since it was the captain's order, there was no need to doubt it.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Hey, the captain said to bring him. Get him in here.
The revolutionary holding the scythe clicked his tongue and roughly shoved Aria aside.
Then he pointed his scythe at Shirone and gestured with a flick of his wrist.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Fine. Walk over here slowly. If you try anything, we'll kill all the hostages.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I know. That's why I'm offering myself as a hostage.
Shirone set the metal box on the ground, trying to avoid any suspicion.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Wait. What's that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is something I purchased from an alchemy shop.
The revolutionary holding the scythe nervously asked.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Fine. Bring that too.
For the Black Revolutionaries, currency was meaningless.
Even a bundle of cash couldn't buy more than an apple on the black market.
But goods were different, especially items ordered by the Magic Association.
Those could likely be traded for a substantial amount of weapons.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Hey, carry that over your head and come here.
Shirone lifted the metal box with both hands and walked towards the Gold Tower.
As soon as he crossed the entrance, three revolutionaries surrounded him.
One snatched the box, another tied his hands behind his back, and the third forced him to his knees, placing a large sword against his neck.

blocks.speaker: Revolutionary
blocks.content: Stay still. Resist, and you die.
Shirone didn't respond, instead surveying his surroundings.
The hostages were gathered in one spot, guarded by three terrorists.
Aria knelt nearby, separated from the group.
Aria, realizing she was alive, looked at Shirone with a mix of emotions.
She felt guilty and astonished to learn he was a Magic Association employee.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: You're a magician?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll be fine. Protect yourself.
The flail-wielding terrorist lost patience and bellowed.

blocks.speaker: Terrorist
blocks.content: Hurry up! Stab him! Before I smash your skull!
As the flail was raised menacingly, Aria burst into tears and brought the dagger toward Shirone's thigh.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: I-I'm sorry… I'm so sorry.
The reversed blade trembled violently.
She couldn't imagine what it would feel like to pierce flesh with cold steel.
Sobbing, Aria pressed the dagger's tip against Shirone's thigh.
Even then, Shirone's expression didn't change.
They wouldn't kill him. He was their most valuable hostage.
You're surrounded! You're surrounded! Release the hostages and surrender immediately! Failure to comply will result in a full-scale assault!
All the terrorists turned their heads toward the windows.
The Thormia Counter-Terrorism Unit had set up barricades and surrounded the Gold Tower.
The elite forces—emergency response units, special suppression squads of magicians, bomb disposal teams of alchemists, and sniper squads of gunners—were all deployed.

blocks.speaker: Terrorist
blocks.content: Damn bastards! If you want to die, die alone!
The unit leader ground his teeth as he glared at the terrorist.
A raid was inevitable, but the timing to minimize casualties was critical.
It could happen now, or in five minutes.
The burden of how much time to give them rested entirely on his shoulders.
A deafening roar tore through the air, and a woman wielding an unusually long staff landed with a thud.
Plu, who had flown directly from the Magic Association, quickly analyzed the situation.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: The counter-terrorism unit has arrived, and all civilians have been evacuated. But where is that guy…?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: The alchemy shop wasn't far, so he should have been nearby, observing the situation. Yet, no matter how hard she looked, Shirone was nowhere to be seen.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What? He didn't come? Did he actually run away? Well, that's better for me…
Her gaze fell on the entrance of the Gold Tower.
When she saw Shirone, held hostage by a terrorist with a resigned expression, her eyes widened, and her jaw dropped.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What the hell are you doing there, you idiot?!
Her outburst drew the counter-terrorism unit's attention.

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: Ah…

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: Who are you? You must be a magician if you teleported here.
Plu, regaining her composure, walked confidently towards him.
Although she wasn't part of the cooperation team, this area was under the jurisdiction of the Magic Association, so she had authority.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I'm Plu, Chief of Security at the Magic Association. What's the current situation... huh?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Oh no, I gave it to that kid.

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: Chief of Security at the Magic Association? Why are you here? We've already assembled the response team.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Ah, well, I thought I might be able to help...
Without her ID, Plu's voice was less confident.

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: Go back. We have enough forces to handle them. The problem is the hostages.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: This is so frustrating. What was he thinking, getting caught by terrorists? Magic doesn't even work there.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Wait, magic?
Maybe Shirone being captured could be an opportunity.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: With a hopeful strategy in mind, Plu pushed through the response team and reached the captain.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Captain! Captain!
The captain sighed and turned around.

blocks.speaker: Captain
blocks.content: What now? We're busy.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Captain! Captain!
ΓÇö
